what_it_does: Implements an HTTP speech-to-text ear on port 2031 that wraps the NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT 0.6B v3 model through the NeMo ASR toolkit, exposing a multipart POST /inference endpoint that returns transcribed text.
observations: Heavy NeMo import is deferred inside _engine() so the module imports cheaply
The model is loaded lazily as a singleton via a global _model variable
Explicit _model.to(DEVICE) is used to force GPU placement rather than rely on NeMo auto-placement
Audio bytes are written to a temporary .wav file because NeMo transcribe() prefers file paths
Environment variables COMPANY_PARAKEET_MODEL and COMPANY_PARAKEET_DEVICE configure model and device
The module mounts the shared _stt_service.serve() runner with a warm=_engine callback
Docstrings contain extensive operational warnings about CUDA-13 compatibility and a fail-loud policy
standouts: Explicit GPU placement guarantee to avoid silent CPU fallback
Guarded heavy import to prevent import-time crashes
Temp-file bridge between in-memory audio bytes and NeMo's path-based transcribe API
Documented fail-loud behavior with no silent fallback to another STT engine
conventions: Follows the project STT-engine pattern of importing and mounting voice.ears._stt_service.serve
Uses environment variables for runtime configuration
Uses lazy singleton initialization for a heavy ML model
Provides a simple bytes-to-dict transcribe(audio: bytes) -> dict contract
concerns: Writing every request to disk adds I/O latency and temporary file management risk
NamedTemporaryFile with delete=True may be unreopenable by path on Windows
No input validation that audio bytes are actually a valid WAV
No explicit concurrency model; the singleton _model may serialize concurrent requests
No graceful error payload in transcribe() beyond raising exceptions
notes: A NeMo-based STT microservice that loads Parakeet lazily, forces GPU placement, and refuses to silently fall back if CUDA or dependencies fail.
questions: How does _stt_service.serve handle failures from warm=_engine?
Is the incoming audio always guaranteed to be a WAV, and who enforces that?
How are concurrent requests serialized or batched around the singleton model?
What NeMo and PyTorch versions were verified on CUDA 13?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: an HTTP STT ear using Parakeet via NeMo on port 2031 with lazy loading and explicit device control.
apparent_themes: Explicit hardware control over implicit defaults
Minimal thin wrapper around a third-party ASR model
intention_signals: Build a self-contained, high-quality STT worker that is robust to silent misplacement onto CPU and clearly signals unavailability when its CUDA/NeMo stack cannot load.
novelty: The explicit _model.to(DEVICE) guard against NeMo's inconsistent auto-placement, plus the documented no-fallback policy tied to registry availability probing.
contribution: A Parakeet/NeMo STT inference worker that exposes a standard HTTP STT endpoint, loads the model on demand, and enforces configured device placement so the rest of the system can treat it as an optional high-accuracy ear.
summary_for_embedding: voice/ears/parakeet.py is a NeMo ASR wrapper that serves NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT 0.6B v3 as an HTTP STT endpoint on port 2031, lazily loading the model and explicitly placing it on the configured device.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a robust, GPU-guaranteed speech-to-text microservice that fails loudly if its heavy NeMo/CUDA stack is unavailable, rather than silently degrading.

symbols.does: Lazily initializes and returns the singleton NeMo ASR model, guarding the heavy import and forcing device placement.
symbols.description: _engine() imports nemo.collections.asr on first call, loads the configured Parakeet model from_pretrained, moves it to the configured DEVICE when CUDA is requested, sets eval mode, and caches it in the global _model variable.

symbols.does: Normalizes a NeMo transcribe result list to a single stripped text string.
symbols.description: _hyp_text(hyps) handles the case where NeMo returns either plain strings or Hypothesis objects, extracting the .text attribute when present and returning an empty string for empty results.

symbols.does: Accepts WAV audio bytes, writes them to a temporary file, runs NeMo transcription, and returns {"text": ...}.
symbols.description: transcribe(audio: bytes) persists the uploaded audio to a temporary .wav file, calls _engine().transcribe([path]), converts the first hypothesis to text via _hyp_text, and returns a dict with the text field.
